summary: This is a brief teaser post directing readers to Joel's full article 'The Law of Leaky Abstractions.' The post introduces the concept that all abstractions leak — they fail to perfectly hide their underlying complexity. Joel argues this leakage happens pervasively and is a drag on software development. The full argument is developed in the linked feature article, which became one of his most influential pieces.
key_points: All abstractions eventually fail to hide their underlying complexity
Leaky abstractions are pervasive across software development
These failures impose real costs and slow down development
money_quotes: Abstractions fail. Sometimes a little, sometimes a lot.
It happens all over the place when you have abstractions, and it's dragging us down.
key_insight: Abstractions inevitably leak, failing to fully hide the complexity beneath them, and this leakage is a fundamental drag on software development.
